mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for future equity cash flows, directly compressing present valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by >0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous peaks in retail sales and housing starts indicate demand exhaustion, causing inventory buildup and subsequent corporate earnings revisions that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint high percentiles of RSAFS and HOUST coincide with positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during monetary tightening regimes, producing negative 20-day returns in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 7.0 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 7.0% are followed by positive 5-day returns in >55% of instances, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for concurrent labor market data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, driving higher consumer spending expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when GDPC1 growth is negative, or the interaction effect yields a Rank IC below 0.03 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue streams, directly supporting equity prices and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth coincides with negative 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening, rising industrial production signals potential overcapacity and inventory accumulation, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods are followed by positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the regime-conditional effect disappears when FEDFUNDS growth is < 0.5% per quarter.
